title: Phlomoides labiosa
taxonomy: Phlomoides labiosa is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. The genus Phlomoides is a subgenus of Phlomis and is native to mountainous regions of Asia. Phlomoides labiosa is a flowering plant that produces small, tubular flowers in shades of pink and purple. It is often found in rocky outcrops and mountain meadows.

intro: Phlomoides labiosa, also known as the labious phlomoides, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides labiosa include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
